text: The Management Processes shall operate on a Linux or Solaris server.
The Management Processes shall be accessible through a web-browser or a Windows-compatible client.
The Management Processes shall be accessible through Microsoft Internet Explorer (v.6.0 and later) and Mozilla Firefox (v.2.0 and later) if the system is web-browser based.
The Management Processes shall be accessible with screen-reading software, screen-magnification software, and other software programs designed to increase accessibility.
The Management Processes shall use a fully relational database back-end.
The Management Processes shall produce standards-compliant HTML.
The Management Processes shall provide a development and training environment with the ability to migrate configurations to a production environment.
The system shall control user rights and privileges through security groups or roles.
The software developer shall provide complete data specifications for authority records, bibliographic records, order records, item records, hold/request records, and other records.
The system shall provide an online, hierarchical, and cross-linked help system in HTML that describes and illustrates all system functions.
The system shall provide high-level error reporting and feedback to users.
The system shall support streamlined staff login methods including card swipes and individual login credentials.
System administrators shall have the ability to create report templates that are available to staff.
The system shall allow administrators to limit the filters and display fields that can be altered in a report template.
The system shall provide distinct permission levels for creating and cloning reports.
System administrators shall have the ability to create shared folders to manage staff access to specific reports.
The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for designing queries against all record types.
The system shall allow staff to select query fields, picklist values, regular expressions, and Boolean operators.
The system shall provide pre-defined reports for library boards including basic statistics and activity reports.
The system shall archive transactions in a form that protects patron privacy according to State Law while providing demographic statistics.
The system shall provide the query selections along with the report results.
The system shall provide the ability to view the status of a report in the processing queue.
The system shall produce statistics to predict patron behavior and use of materials.
The system shall produce anonymized demographic statistics including transactions by geographical region and age range.
The system shall report on the volume of material in a library based on new acquisitions and items in circulation.
The system shall provide an interface showing the capacity of all library branches.
The system shall compare the percentage of total circulations, percentage of collection, and percentage of total shelf space for each genre and format.
The system shall support inventory control and transaction counting for uncataloged material.
The system shall provide a utility for transferring batches of items between branches.
The system shall provide reports of missing and damaged items per branch.
The system shall generate a list of items that have been in-transit for more than a configurable number of days.
The system shall provide a utility for identifying and purging item records based on customizable criteria.
The system shall count and track record deletions per location and per system.
The system shall allow queries and reports to be limited or grouped by patron characteristics.
The system shall generate a list of patrons with no activity in a configurable number of days.
The system shall maintain transaction history for a configurable number of days.
The system shall maintain monthly and annual aggregate information indefinitely.
The system shall count all types of check-in and check-out individually and cumulatively.
The system shall count all hold requests by the method used to place the hold.
The system shall generate a report of the monetary value of items in the collection.
All reports and data archiving must comply with standard accounting practice and auditing requirements.
The system shall track fines waived and payments made per library.
The system shall provide financial reports including account balances and charges accrued per time period.
The system shall maintain a ledger of patron payments to facilitate financial reconciliation.
The system shall provide the ability to send reports to specified email addresses.
The system shall provide the ability to cancel a report that is scheduled or currently running.
The system shall support real-time reporting.
The system shall include all null and blank fields in report results regardless of filters.
